name: ESP32 BLE Collector
label: ESP32 BLE Collector (tobozo) — passive BLE advert logger to SD
description: tobozo/ESP32-BLECollector — a passive Bluetooth LE scanner that logs every nearby BLE advertisement (name, MAC, vendor, RSSI) to an SD card, with an on-device UI. Passive receive only — no transmit, pairing, or attack. *** APP-ONLY UPDATE: the upstream release ships only the compiled app image (no bootloader/partition table). Cyber Controller flashes it at 0x10000 as an app UPDATE over your device's existing M5Stack/Odroid factory bootloader; it does NOT full-flash a blank board and requires a large-app partition scheme (the app is ~1.1-1.9 MB — 'No OTA (Large Apps)' / 'Minimal SPIFFS'). For a blank board or a small-partition device, flash via M5Burner (which bundles the bootloader+partitions) first. *** Cyber Controller adds no capability of its own.

firmware_urls.note: Arduino project; the release ships one compiled APP image per board (<Board>-BLECollector.bin, an esp-idf v4.4.4 app). Pinned + SHA-256-verified at flash time, never vendored.

assets.name: S3Box-BLECollector.bin
assets.label: M5Stack CoreS3 / ESP32-S3-BOX — BLE Collector app

note: APP-ONLY firmware — verified via esptool image-info: each <Board>-BLECollector.bin is an esp-idf v4.4.4 APPLICATION image (has App Information), NOT a merged bin and NOT a bootloader; there is NO bootloader/partition-table in the release. So this is an app UPDATE flashed at 0x10000 over the device's factory bootloader (flash_mode='app' -> CC writes only the app; a full-flash correctly refuses since no boot chain is provided). REAL SHA-256 computed from the downloaded 1.6.1 assets (5 boards) so verify_sha256 passes. Chips confirmed: M5Core2/M5Fire/M5stack/OdroidGo = classic ESP32; S3Box = ESP32-S3 (image-info Chip ID 9). RELIABILITY caveat: the app is ~1.1-1.9 MB and needs a large-app partition scheme ('No OTA (Large Apps)' / 'Minimal SPIFFS') already on the device; flashing over a small default app0 partition would overflow into adjacent partitions and fail to boot (recoverable by re-flashing via M5Burner, not a brick). Repo now MIT (shortlist's 'no license' note superseded). v1.6.1 (2023-05, stable/stale). Offset 0x10000 is the ESP-IDF/Arduino standard; treat-as-verify: until a real M5Stack flash confirms boot (Phase-F HW gate).
